incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the groups first, then count how many dots are in each group.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt

steps.text: Let's count together. I see 6 groups. Each group has 2 dots. Place those two factors first, then skip-count by 2s six times to find the product.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the correct equation.

params.description: Tiles place into slots in sequence: 6 in first slot, 2 in second slot, 12 in product slot.

steps.text: Here's how to build it. First I count the groups: 6 groups total. That's the first factor. Then I count the dots in each group: 2 dots. That's the second factor. Now I skip-count by 2s six times: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. The product is 12. The complete equation is 6 times 2 equals 12.
